item.name: BadgerChat Mini

request.description: Logs a user in. BadgerChat Mini does not require pre-registration — any username/password creates a session. Sets the `ice_auth` cookie on success.
Clears the `ice_auth` cookie, ending the current session.
Returns the most recent comments on BadgerChat Mini's single board. Public — no login required.
Posts a new comment. Requires a valid `ice_auth` session (run the Login request first). Max 512 characters.

item.description: The four `/rest/s26/ice/*` endpoints your tools front. Run Login first so `ice_auth` is set; then Create Comment will be authorized.

item.name: AI Responses

request.description: A plain chat turn with no tools. Returns `{ "msg": "..." }`.
A plain chat turn with no tools. Returns `{ "msg": "..." }`.
A plain chat turn with no tools. Returns `{ "msg": "..." }`.

Sends tool definitions to the model. The model should respond with `{ "tool_calls": [{ "call_id": "...", "name": "get_comments", "arguments": { ... } }] }` rather than a `msg`. `arguments` is a JSON object. Copy the `call_id` into the next request.

After the model asks for a tool call, your app executes the tool and feeds the result back as a `function_call` + `function_call_output` pair (matched by `call_id`). The model then produces a natural-language summary. This is the loop your `handleSend` drives up to `MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS` times.

item.description: The shared AI Responses endpoint (same one HW11 uses). Run Basic Chat to see plain message flow, Tool Call Request to see the model choose a tool, and Agentic Loop to see how your app feeds the tool's output back.
